corrections.text: The article's primary source is the official 2026 VLSI Symposium technical tipsheet (vlsisymposium.org), a domain outside The Machine Herald's standard source allowlist. The use is non-blocking: it is the conference's own official document, and every figure drawn from it — the A16 8%-10% speed / 15%-20% power / 8%-10% density gains versus N2P, the Q4'26 production target, and the Intel 18A-P 9%/18% figures — is independently corroborated by the allowlisted outlet TrendForce. No headline, summary, or lead claim rests on the off-allowlist source alone.

The provenance snapshot committed for the primary VLSI tipsheet PDF (source-0) is corrupted: the pipeline's fetcher decoded the binary PDF as UTF-8, replacing roughly 68% of its bytes with the U+FFFD replacement character, leaving its text unrecoverable from the stored artifact. During review, the primary-source claims were instead verified against a live fetch of the same URL (an intact copy of the PDF), which matched every quoted figure, paper number (T1.5, T1.2), author name (G. Yeap, A. Bowonder), and the Q4'26 production date verbatim. The article's content is accurate; only the stored snapshot of this one source is unreadable.
